sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Everton are showing decent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 2 wins and 3 draws. Bournemouth are also in strong form, unbeaten in their last 5 with 2 wins and 3 draws, highlighted by a thrilling 3-2 victory over Liverpool. Both teams are performing well, setting the stage for a competitive encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results have been split with Everton winning 1-0 away in December 2025 and Bournemouth securing a convincing 3-0 victory at Everton in July 2025. Historically, Bournemouth have dominated with 4 wins in the last 5 encounters against Everton, which could give them a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Everton, sitting 8th with 37 points, have a balanced record with 28 goals scored and conceded, showcasing defensive solidity (4th fewest goals conceded). Bournemouth, in 11th with 34 points, have been more prolific in attack with 41 goals but vulnerable defensively with 44 conceded. This suggests Everton might focus on defense while Bournemouth could push for goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Everton’s James Garner (7.84 average rating in last 5 matches) will be crucial in midfield, while the absence of Jack Grealish could impact their creativity. Bournemouth’s Eli Junior Kroupi (8 goals this season) and in-form Rayan (7.7 rating) are key threats, though missing Marcus Tavernier may hurt their attack. Both teams have talent to exploit weaknesses.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Everton’s strength lies in late-game scoring (35.7% of goals after the 76th minute) and aerial duels (3rd in league). Bournemouth also score heavily late (29.5% after 76th minute) and excel in creating corners (average over 10.5 per game recently). Expect a physical game with a focus on set-pieces and late drama.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the close Sofascore probabilities (38% home win, 25% draw, 37% away win) and recent form, this match is likely to be tightly contested. Bournemouth’s historical edge and attacking output slightly favor them, but Everton’s home advantage and defensive organization could balance things out. A draw seems a plausible outcome with both teams scoring.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Everton lead Bournemouth by a narrow 1-0 margin at Hill Dickinson Stadium in Liverpool. The sole goal came from a penalty converted by Iliman Ndiaye just before the break, giving the home side a crucial advantage. Despite Bournemouth controlling possession and showing more presence in the final third, Everton capitalized on their opportunity to take the lead. Both teams have had their moments, but the game remains finely poised heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Everton have been clinical when it mattered, converting one of their big chances into a goal despite having less possession than Bournemouth. Their defense, led by James Tarkowski and Jarrad Branthwaite, has been solid with multiple clearances to thwart Bournemouth's attacks. Bournemouth, on the other hand, have dominated possession and made frequent entries into the final third and penalty area but failed to create any significant scoring opportunities. Their aerial dominance and duel-winning ability have been notable, yet they lack the cutting edge in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For Everton, Iliman Ndiaye has been the standout performer, not only scoring the penalty but also posing a constant threat with his positioning and shots on target. Defensively, Tarkowski and Branthwaite have been pivotal in keeping Bournemouth at bay. On the Bournemouth side, Evanilson has been active with a couple of shots on target, while Alex Jimenez has contributed both defensively and in attack. However, no Bournemouth player has managed to truly unlock Everton's defense in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Bournemouth showing early intent, maintaining attacking pressure for the initial minutes. Everton responded with a shift in momentum around the midway point of the half, building up pressure and creating dangerous moments. Bournemouth regained some control as the half progressed, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells. However, Everton seized a critical moment late in the half to secure the lead. The momentum has swung back and forth, with both teams having their share of attacking phases, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Bournemouth come out aggressively as they chase an equalizer, leveraging their possession and final third presence from the first half. Everton will aim to protect their lead, potentially sitting deeper and looking for counter-attacking opportunities through players like Ndiaye. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with Bournemouth possibly introducing fresh legs to increase their attacking output. Everton, meanwhile, may focus on tightening their defensive structure to frustrate the away side. Expect a competitive half with chances at both ends.
